title: Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
summary: A framework for reinforcement learning that involves multiple agents interacting with each other. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Multi-Agent Systems. Start after Reinforcement Learning Agent and Learning Agent. It opens into Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) for Control.

blocks.title: Compare and Contrast

panels.label: What it is
panels.body: A framework for reinforcement learning that involves multiple agents interacting with each other.

panels.label: What it is not
panels.body: It is not just Competitive Learning and Cooperative-Autonomous Swarms; the graph shows how it sits inside Reinforcement Learning.

panels.label: Common confusion
panels.body: Ask whether the term changes policy, reward, exploration, or control dynamics. Compare it against Competitive Learning and Cooperative-Autonomous Swarms so the boundary stays visible instead of collapsing into a synonym pile.

panels.label: Best next step
panels.body: Review Reinforcement Learning Agent and Learning Agent first, then move toward Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) for Control.

blocks.takeaway: The comparison view keeps Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning honest by showing the nearest neighbors, the real boundary, and the next study move.
